tasks.title: Implement resolve-or-create for campaign/batch slugs
tasks.description:
In `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-draft-ingestion.service.ts`, replace the resolve-only `resolveSlugToId` call for `campaignSlug`/`batchSlug` with a resolve-or-create helper: match by slug → associate; no match → create a new `social_campaigns`/`social_content_batches` row (name derived from the slug, `active: true`) and associate to it. Leave `audienceSlug`/`footerSlug`/`baseHashtagSetSlug` on the existing resolve-only path — only campaigns/batches get create semantics (G-4/G-5). Return `{ id, slug, isNew }` per resolved entity so callers can report the outcome.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests in `social-draft-ingestion.service.test.ts`: existing slug resolves without creating a row; unknown slug creates a new active row and returns `isNew: true`; audience/footer/hashtagSet slugs still fall back to null on miss (regression).

subtasks.title: Add resolveOrCreateCampaignOrBatch helper
Wire campaignSlug/batchSlug through the new helper
Unit test: existing slug resolves, no create
Unit test: unknown slug creates row with isNew=true

tasks.title: Echo campaign/batch resolution outcome in draft response
tasks.description:
Extend `CreateSocialDraftResponseSchema` (`packages/schemas/src/entities/social/social-draft.http.schema.ts`, additive-only per the schema-compat policy) with optional `campaignResolution`/`batchResolution: { id, slug, isNew } | null` fields. Wire `SocialDraftIngestionService.ingestDraft` to populate them from T-001's helper output so the GPT/operator can confirm whether a new campaign/batch was created.

Test requirements:
- Schema unit test: new fields parse as optional, absent = backward compatible with existing historic fixtures.
- Ingestion service test: response includes correct `isNew` value for both existing-slug and new-slug cases.

subtasks.title: Add campaignResolution/batchResolution to response schema
Populate fields in ingestDraft from T-001 helper
Schema compat test against historic fixtures

tasks.title: Update GPT-facing route descriptions for fuzzy-duplicate + implicit fallback
tasks.description:
Update the OpenAPI `description` text on `POST /api/v1/ai/social/drafts` (`apps/api/src/routes/ai/social/drafts.ts`) and `GET /api/v1/ai/social/catalog` (`apps/api/src/routes/ai/social/catalog.ts`) — these descriptions are the Custom GPT's own instructions (NG-2: no backend heuristic matching, so this is the only implementation surface for G-5's LLM-reasoning requirement). Instruct: (1) before submitting a new `campaignSlug`/`batchSlug` name, check the catalog's `campaigns`/`batches` arrays for a near-duplicate name and ask the operator to confirm "use existing" vs "create new"; (2) when no explicit slug is given, reason over the active campaigns/batches list against the draft content and propose an association when confident.

Test requirements:
- Update `apps/api/test/routes/social/admin/social-gpt-action-schema.test.ts` (or the drafts/catalog route test) to assert the generated OpenAPI description contains the new guidance text.

subtasks.title: Update drafts.ts route description
Update catalog.ts route description
Test: OpenAPI schema exposes updated description

tasks.title: Integration tests for batch/campaign auto-detection acceptance criteria
tasks.description:
Write integration tests against `POST /api/v1/ai/social/drafts` covering AC-1/AC-2/AC-3: (1) an explicit new campaign name creates a row and associates the draft; (2) an explicit existing slug associates without creating a duplicate; (3) a draft submitted with no explicit campaign/batch reference ships unassociated at the backend level (the implicit-fallback reasoning itself is GPT-side per T-003, so this test only verifies the backend's pass-through behavior when no slug is sent).

Test requirements:
- Add cases to the existing drafts route test file. Use realistic near-duplicate names ("Lanzamiento 2026" vs "Lanzamiento 26") as fixture data for AC-2's manual/GPT-side verification note in the test comment.

subtasks.title: Test: explicit new name creates + associates
Test: explicit existing slug associates only
Test: no explicit slug ships unassociated

tasks.title: Add date-range filtering to dashboard service
tasks.description:
Extend `GetDashboardInput`/`SocialPostService.getDashboard()` in `packages/service-core/src/services/social/social-post.service.ts` with optional `dateFrom`/`dateTo` fields, filtering the KPI counters, quick-approval queue, and recent-failures queries by that window when provided. No range = current all-time behavior (regression-safe default).

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: KPI counts change correctly for a bounded range; omitting the range preserves existing unfiltered totals (explicit regression assertion).

subtasks.title: Add dateFrom/dateTo to GetDashboardInput
Apply range filter to KPI/queue/failures queries
Unit test: bounded range
Unit test: no range = unchanged totals

tasks.title: Add per-platform breakdown aggregation to dashboard service
tasks.description:
Add a `platformBreakdown: { platform, count }[]` field to `SocialDashboardData` (`social-post.service.ts`), computed by grouping `social_post_targets` by platform within the (optional) date range from T-005.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: breakdown counts sum to the expected total for a fixture set; empty-data case returns an empty array, not an error.

subtasks.title: Add platformBreakdown to SocialDashboardData
Implement grouped aggregation query
Unit test: breakdown sums correctly

tasks.title: Wire dashboard route + schema for date range and platform breakdown
tasks.description:
Extend the dashboard's request/response Zod schema (additive) with optional `dateFrom`/`dateTo` query params and the `platformBreakdown` response field. Update `apps/api/src/routes/social/admin/dashboard/get.ts` to parse the query params and pass them to `getDashboard()`.

Test requirements:
- Route test: query params are parsed and forwarded to the service; response includes `platformBreakdown`; omitted params keep current behavior.

subtasks.title: Extend dashboard schema (additive)
Wire get.ts to parse dateFrom/dateTo query params
Route test: params forwarded + response shape

tasks.title: Ensure admin posts list response exposes thumbnailUrl
tasks.description:
Verify whether `GET /api/v1/admin/social/posts` (`apps/api/src/routes/social/admin/posts/`) already returns a thumbnail URL per post (`social_post_media`). If missing, add it to the list response schema and query (additive). This is the backend prerequisite for the posts-table thumbnail column (T-011).

Test requirements:
- Route/unit test asserting each list item includes `thumbnailUrl` (string URL or null when no media exists).

subtasks.title: Check current admin posts list response shape
Add thumbnailUrl to list schema/query if missing
Test: thumbnailUrl present for posts with/without media

tasks.title: Add date-range picker to admin dashboard page
tasks.description:
Add a date-range picker control to `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/social/index.tsx`, wired to the dashboard data-fetching hook so KPIs, quick-approval queue, and recent failures re-fetch with the selected `dateFrom`/`dateTo` from T-007.

Test requirements:
- Component/route test in `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/social/__tests__/dashboard.test.tsx`: changing the date range triggers a refetch with the correct query params; default (no range) still renders current totals.

subtasks.title: Add date-range picker control
Wire picker state to dashboard query hook
Test: range change triggers correct refetch

tasks.title: Add per-platform breakdown chart to dashboard
tasks.description:
Add a per-platform breakdown chart component to the admin social dashboard, reusing the project's existing charting library per the `dataviz` skill conventions, rendering the `platformBreakdown` array from T-007.

Test requirements:

tasks.title: Add thumbnail column to social posts table
tasks.description:

Test requirements:

Add fallback placeholder for missing thumbnail
Component test: both states

tasks.title: Verify dashboard AC-4 end-to-end
tasks.description:
Write an integration-level test verifying AC-4: selecting a date range shows correct KPI numbers for that range and a per-platform breakdown that sums to the range total, exercising the service, route, and rendered UI together.

Test requirements:
- Cross-layer test (service → route → dashboard component) confirming the breakdown-sums-to-total invariant holds for a seeded fixture set across at least two date ranges.

subtasks.title: Seed fixture posts/targets across two date windows
Assert breakdown sums to range total

tasks.title: Scaffold admin compose-social-post page
tasks.description:
⚠ BLOCKED — depends on HOS-65 (Publishing Engine Extension, currently Backlog) shipping the API-side multi-target fan-out contract. Do not start until HOS-65's target-row creation endpoint exists.

Scaffold a new admin route (e.g. `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/social/compose/index.tsx`) for manually authoring a social post: title, caption, pillar — no platform selection yet (that's T-014). No admin composer UI exists today (verified — the only creation path currently is the Custom GPT); this is genuinely new UI surface.

Test requirements:
- Route test: page renders, base form fields validate required inputs.

Test requirements:

Write an end-to-end test verifying AC-5: selecting 3 platforms in the composer and saving creates exactly 3 `social_post_targets` rows in the database.

Test requirements:

Assert caption overrides persist per target

tasks.title: Define public-data-pull response schema
tasks.description:
Add a new additive schema (`@repo/schemas`) for a draft-enrichment public-data-pull endpoint: an array of `{ entityType, id, title, slug, summary, imageUrl }` items. Scope tightly per R-1 (avoid a general-purpose public API aggregator) — this schema covers ONLY the entity shape needed for draft enrichment, not a general public API response.

Test requirements:
- Schema unit test validating the shape and that `entityType` is constrained to a small explicit enum (start with `ACCOMMODATION`, `DESTINATION`).

subtasks.title: Define enrichment item schema + entityType enum
Schema unit test

tasks.title: Implement public-data aggregation service
tasks.description:
Implement an aggregation method (new or on an existing service) that queries a tightly-scoped set of public entities — start with recently-published/featured accommodations and destinations ONLY (per R-1, do not add more entity types speculatively) — shaped per T-021's schema for draft enrichment. Accept an optional free-text query param to narrow results.

Test requirements:
- Unit tests: returns accommodations + destinations shaped correctly; free-text query narrows results; empty result set handled gracefully (no error).

subtasks.title: Implement aggregation query for accommodations + destinations
Support optional free-text narrowing param
Unit tests: shape, narrowing, empty state

tasks.title: Add public-data-pull API route
tasks.description:
Add a new route (`apps/api/src/routes/ai/social/public-data.ts`, following the `createApiKeyRoute` pattern used by `catalog.ts`/`drafts.ts` since it is GPT-consumed for draft enrichment) wiring T-022's service, authenticated the same way as the other `/api/v1/ai/social/*` routes.

Test requirements:
- Route test: auth (x-hospeda-ai-key) required; successful call returns the expected shape; free-text query param forwarded correctly.

subtasks.title: Create public-data.ts route
Register route under /api/v1/ai/social
Route test: auth + shape + query param

tasks.title: Verify public-data-pull scoping and auth end-to-end
tasks.description:

Test requirements:
- Integration test asserting only accommodations/destinations are returned (no unexpected entity types) and a missing/invalid API key yields 401.

subtasks.title: Test: only scoped entity types returned
Test: missing/invalid api key → 401

Test requirements:

tasks.title: Document campaign/batch resolve-or-create and public-data-pull scope
tasks.description:
Document (in `apps/api/CLAUDE.md` or a relevant `docs/` guide) the new resolve-or-create behavior for campaign/batch slugs (NG-2: matching stays LLM-side, never add backend keyword/hashtag heuristics) and the intentionally narrow scope of the public-data-pull endpoint (R-1: accommodations + destinations only — extend deliberately, not speculatively).

Test requirements: none (documentation-only task).
